housing.stated_position: Vance attributed high housing costs primarily to illegal immigration and a housing supply shortage, though fact-checkers found his immigration figures inaccurate; he has not proposed specific rent control measures.
housing.direct_quote: A lot of young people are saying, housing is way too expensive. Why is that? Because we flooded the country with 30 million illegal immigrants who were taking houses that ought by right go to American citizens. And at the same time we weren't building enough new houses to begin with even for the population that we had.

healthcare.stated_position: Vance supports Medicare drug price negotiation and protecting pre-existing condition coverage, while advocating for healthcare deregulation to promote more consumer choice—diverging from traditional GOP orthodoxy in some respects.
healthcare.direct_quote: I think we have to let Medicare negotiate prescription drug prices so that our seniors aren't paying through the roof for prescription drugs.

aipac_bds.stated_position: Vance has consistently supported strong U.S.-Israel ties and endorsed anti-BDS policies, aligned with the Trump administration's stance conditioning federal funding on anti-boycott compliance.

minimum_wage.stated_position: Vance co-sponsored a Republican bill in 2023 to gradually raise the federal minimum wage to $11 per hour by 2028, departing from conservative orthodoxy that opposes any federal minimum wage increase.

student_debt.stated_position: Vance strongly opposed Biden's student debt cancellation, calling it a windfall for the rich and college-educated at the expense of working-class taxpayers, and argued Republicans must fight such relief 'with every ounce of our energy and power.'
student_debt.direct_quote: Forgiving student debt is a massive windfall to the rich, to the college educated, and most of all to the corrupt university administrators of America. Republicans must fight this with every ounce of our energy and power.

healthcare.stated_position: Rubio has called for repealing the Affordable Care Act and replacing it with tax credits, high-risk pools for pre-existing conditions, expanded health savings accounts, and per-capita block grants to states for Medicaid.

aipac_bds.stated_position: Rubio reintroduced the Combating BDS Act of 2023, calling BDS 'the single most destructive campaign of economic warfare against the Jewish state of Israel' and calling it 'anti-Semitism disguised as economic policy.'
aipac_bds.direct_quote: The BDS movement is the single most destructive campaign of economic warfare against the Jewish state of Israel. This bill, which previously passed the Senate, would mark an important step toward bringing an end to the movement's discriminatory efforts.

minimum_wage.stated_position: Rubio has opposed federal minimum wage hikes, instead proposing to replace the Earned Income Tax Credit with a federal wage enhancement for low-wage workers—arguing this is a better way to support low-income workers than simply raising the minimum wage.

student_debt.stated_position: Rubio proposed a higher education reform plan centered on income-share agreements (investors fund college in exchange for a percentage of income), expanded vocational training, and full disclosure of expected post-graduation earnings—opposing broad federal debt cancellation.

aipac_bds.stated_position: Cruz is a strong supporter of Israel and unconditionally backs Israel's military operations, stating the U.S. supports Israel because it is in America's national security interest and calling on Congress to provide military aid for as long as needed to defeat Hamas.
aipac_bds.direct_quote: Israel is one of our most critical allies, and it is fighting an existential war being waged against it by Iran and the terrorists Iran controls across the Middle East. Republicans must have the moral clarity and unity that is sorely lacking in the Democrat Party. We stand united behind our ally, Israel, and we are committed to providing Israel with the military support it needs for as long as it takes to utterly defeat Hamas.

minimum_wage.stated_position: Cruz has opposed federal minimum wage increases, introducing an amendment in 2014 to replace minimum wage legislation with his energy development bill, arguing that raising the minimum wage 'kills jobs.'
minimum_wage.direct_quote: The Congressional Budget Office estimates that raising the minimum wage could cause the loss of up to one million jobs.

student_debt.stated_position: Cruz called Biden's student debt cancellation 'morally bankrupt' and 'illegal,' arguing it forces blue-collar workers to subsidize Washington bureaucrats' college degrees and will cost every taxpayer an average of $2,100.
student_debt.direct_quote: This administration's policy is to force blue collar workers and American families across the country to pay off the cost of a Washington bureaucrat's college degree – it's morally bankrupt. Let's be clear - there is no way to 'cancel' student debt. This will cost every taxpayer an average of $2100.

healthcare.stated_position: McConnell led repeated efforts to repeal and replace the Affordable Care Act and has opposed Medicare for All and Medicaid expansion, arguing government-run healthcare leads to worse outcomes and higher costs.

aipac_bds.stated_position: McConnell attended the AIPAC conference and has consistently supported strong U.S.-Israel relations, backing anti-BDS legislation and military aid to Israel.

minimum_wage.stated_position: McConnell has blocked federal minimum wage increases throughout his Senate leadership tenure, opposing the Raise the Wage Act and arguing such mandates harm small businesses and reduce employment.

student_debt.stated_position: McConnell called Biden's student debt cancellation 'student loan socialism' and 'astonishingly unfair,' arguing it disproportionately benefits higher-income Americans and forces working families to subsidize others' college degrees.
student_debt.direct_quote: President Biden's student loan socialism is a slap in the face to every family who sacrificed to save for college, every graduate who paid their debt, and every American who chose a certain career path or volunteered to serve in our Armed Forces in order to avoid taking on debt. This policy is astonishingly unfair.

housing.stated_position: Hawley introduced the Homes for American Families Act with Sen. Merkley to prohibit large institutional investors from buying single-family homes, arguing Wall Street has exploited the housing crisis by converting the nation's housing stock into rental portfolios.
housing.direct_quote: Wall Street has exploited the American housing crisis, turning the nation's housing stock into a portfolio of rental properties. Families deserve to be able to buy their own homes and achieve the American dream without competing with big investment companies that irrevocably drive up housing prices.

minimum_wage.stated_position: Hawley introduced the Higher Wages for American Workers Act to raise the federal minimum wage to $15 per hour starting in 2026, indexed to inflation—an unusual position for a conservative Republican—arguing blue-collar workers deserve a long-overdue raise.
minimum_wage.direct_quote: For decades, working Americans have seen their wages flatline. The federal minimum wage currently sits at $7.25 per hour and has not risen since 2009.

student_debt.stated_position: Hawley introduced the Make the Universities Pay Act, requiring universities to cover 50% of defaulted student loans and allowing student debt to be discharged in bankruptcy—focusing on holding institutions accountable rather than broad federal cancellation.

housing.stated_position: Trump proposed banning large institutional investors from buying single-family homes, opening federal lands for housing construction, cutting regulations, and reducing inflation to lower mortgage rates, while proposing deep cuts to HUD rental assistance programs.
housing.direct_quote: People live in homes, not corporations.

healthcare.stated_position: Trump called on Congress to enact his 'Great Healthcare Plan,' which would lower drug prices, reduce insurance premiums, and send cost-sharing reduction funds directly to eligible Americans to buy insurance of their choice—stopping short of Medicare expansion.

aipac_bds.stated_position: Trump signed an executive order expanding anti-semitism enforcement and required entities receiving federal funding to certify they are not boycotting Israeli companies, conditioning disaster preparedness grants on anti-BDS compliance.
aipac_bds.direct_quote: My promise to Jewish Americans is this: With your vote, I will be your defender, your protector, and I will be the best friend Jewish Americans have ever had in the White House.

minimum_wage.stated_position: Trump rescinded Biden's $15 minimum wage executive order for federal contractors in March 2025 and has not committed to a national minimum wage increase, though he said in late 2024 he would 'consider' raising it without specifying an amount.

student_debt.stated_position: Trump's administration ended the Biden SAVE repayment plan, resumed collections including wage garnishment for defaulted borrowers, and moved to settle litigation that would require Education Dept. to follow statutory debt cancellation rules under existing programs.
student_debt.direct_quote: The law is clear: if you take out a loan, you must pay it back.

housing.stated_position: McCarthy's debt ceiling deal with Biden resulted in spending caps that the National Low Income Housing Coalition warned would cause tens of thousands of families to lose rental assistance; he has not championed expanded housing investment.

healthcare.stated_position: McCarthy's debt ceiling proposal included work requirements for Medicaid recipients, seeking to restrict access to government healthcare programs for those not meeting employment criteria.

aipac_bds.stated_position: McCarthy blocked a Capitol Visitor Center event on the Palestinian Nakba in May 2023, hosting instead a bipartisan event honoring the 75th anniversary of the U.S.-Israel relationship, consistent with his longstanding strong pro-Israel stance.
aipac_bds.direct_quote: This event in the US Capitol is canceled. Instead, I will host a bipartisan discussion to honor the 75th anniversary of the US-Israel relationship.

student_debt.stated_position: McCarthy's debt ceiling deal included a provision requiring the Biden administration to end the student loan payment pause, and House Republicans under his leadership demanded oversight hearings on Biden's student debt relief plan, calling it illegal.
student_debt.direct_quote: This student loan debt scheme is not a legal or responsible policy, and the chaos the administration has created is unacceptable.

housing.stated_position: DeSantis signed the Live Local Act, a $711 million housing investment package to expand affordable housing, workforce housing, and down payment assistance—the largest housing investment in Florida history—focused on supply-side solutions without rent control.
housing.direct_quote: This legislation provides record support for Florida's workforce and their families to help them live where they work, while also providing historic support for our military communities and the families living near military bases.

healthcare.stated_position: DeSantis signed a major healthcare workforce bill investing $717 million to improve access to care and expand residency slots, but has consistently rejected Medicaid expansion under the ACA for Florida.

minimum_wage.stated_position: DeSantis signed Florida's Medicaid budget requiring Medicaid providers to pay employees at least $15/hour, though he has opposed a federal $15 minimum wage mandate as a one-size-fits-all approach harmful to small businesses.

student_debt.stated_position: DeSantis proposed as part of his 2024 presidential platform to make universities—not taxpayers—responsible for student loan defaults, and to allow student debt to be discharged through bankruptcy like any other debt, opposing broad federal cancellation.
student_debt.direct_quote: I have sympathy for some of these students because I think they were sold a bill of goods.

housing.stated_position: Sanders supports strong national rent control standards, endorsing California's statewide rent control ballot measure and advocating that landlords should not be free to raise rents to whatever level they choose.
housing.direct_quote: Landlords should not be allowed to raise rents to whatever they want, whenever they want. Big Oil should not be allowed to make record profits while drilling near neighborhoods, daycare centers, and schools and endangering people's health.

healthcare.stated_position: Sanders is the lead Senate sponsor of Medicare for All legislation, calling for replacing private insurance with a universal, government-funded single-payer system covering all Americans.

aipac_bds.stated_position: Sanders called on all Democrats to reject AIPAC funding, describing the organization as one that helped deliver the presidency to Donald Trump, and introduced Senate resolutions to block offensive arms sales to Israel citing the humanitarian crisis in Gaza.
aipac_bds.direct_quote: No Democrat should accept money from AIPAC — an organization that also helped deliver the presidency to Donald Trump.

minimum_wage.stated_position: Sanders introduced the Raise the Wage Act to raise the federal minimum wage to $17 per hour by 2030, calling the current $7.25 wage a 'starvation wage' and arguing every worker deserves a living wage.
minimum_wage.direct_quote: The $7.25 an hour minimum wage is a starvation wage. It must be raised to a living wage – at least $17 an hour.

student_debt.stated_position: Sanders supports canceling all $1.6 trillion in outstanding federal student debt and making public colleges tuition-free, arguing the burden of student debt disproportionately harms working families.

housing.stated_position: Harris proposed building 3 million new housing units over four years, a $40 billion innovation fund for local housing solutions, $25,000 in down payment assistance for first-time buyers, and limiting excessive rent increases by corporate landlords.

healthcare.stated_position: Harris moved away from Medicare for All as the 2024 Democratic nominee, instead pledging to expand and strengthen the Affordable Care Act, extend the $35 insulin cap to all Americans, and allow Medicare to continue negotiating drug prices.
healthcare.direct_quote: I will make affordable health care a right, not a privilege by expanding and strengthening the Affordable Care Act and making permanent the Biden-Harris tax credit enhancements that are lowering health care premiums by an average of about $800 a year.

minimum_wage.stated_position: Harris supported a $15 federal minimum wage as a senator and as Vice President, voting for the American Rescue Plan which included the wage increase provision (later stripped), and championed broader Biden-era labor policies.

student_debt.stated_position: Harris was a key internal advocate for Biden's broad student debt cancellation plan and stated she would continue expanding forgiveness programs, including relief for public servants and those who have carried debt for 20+ years.
student_debt.direct_quote: We see a future where every student has the support and the resources they need to thrive. And a future where no teacher has to struggle with the burden of student loan debt.

housing.stated_position: Warren co-authored the bipartisan 21st Century ROAD to Housing Act, which passed the Senate 89-10, aiming to expand housing supply, reduce regulations, and prohibit large institutional investors from acquiring single-family homes.
housing.direct_quote: For the first time, this will shift housing prices in a more favorable direction and push private equity out of the equation—making a clear statement that homes are meant for the families who reside in them, not for Wall Street investors seeking profit.

healthcare.stated_position: Warren has supported Medicare for All and expanding coverage, and has consistently opposed Republican efforts to roll back the Affordable Care Act or cut Medicaid and Medicare funding.

minimum_wage.stated_position: Warren co-sponsored the Raise the Wage Act and has consistently supported raising the federal minimum wage to at least $15, arguing that working people deserve wages that allow them to live with dignity.

student_debt.stated_position: Warren has been one of the most vocal Senate advocates for sweeping student debt cancellation, co-leading letters urging the Biden administration to use executive authority to cancel up to $50,000 per borrower and pushing for expanded eligibility.
student_debt.direct_quote: 20 million Americans will never have to make another student loan payment. Another 23 million Americans will have significant relief on their student loans. This is about helping America's middle class and providing relief to those who need it most.

housing.stated_position: Booker has advocated for housing affordability measures and supported legislation addressing the housing crisis, framing it as connected to broader issues of wage stagnation and student debt.

aipac_bds.stated_position: Booker has consistently opposed the BDS movement, co-sponsoring the Israel Anti-Boycott Act, saying he has never and will never support BDS, and arguing that the movement undermines the prospects for a two-state solution.
aipac_bds.direct_quote: I do not support BDS. Never have, never will. There are some in the BDS movement that don't believe Israel should even exist. It's an anti-Israel movement. I believe a lot of things that BDS is calling for actually hurt our ability to get a two-state solution.

minimum_wage.stated_position: Booker co-sponsored the Raise the Wage Act of 2025 to raise the federal minimum wage to $17 by 2030, consistently supporting increases to the federal minimum wage as part of a broader pro-worker agenda.

student_debt.stated_position: Booker has supported student debt relief efforts and called for addressing the crushing burden of student loans on young Americans, framing it as part of an economic justice agenda.

housing.stated_position: Schumer laid out a Democratic housing agenda called 'Opportunity Starts At Home,' calling for measures to reduce rent, boost homeownership, stop predatory corporate investors, and create an ARPA-style innovation fund for housing.
housing.direct_quote: Democrats are going to focus on costs like a laser in 2026 and beyond. A Democratic Majority will tackle the housing affordability crisis by working to reduce rent, boost homeownership, stop predatory corporation, supercharge construction, and provide housing security for all Americans.

healthcare.stated_position: Schumer announced that Democrats would make healthcare and the rising cost of living the top political issues for 2026, criticizing the Trump administration's policies as driving up healthcare costs for working families.
healthcare.direct_quote: Democrats are going to make health care and other high costs, the high cost of living, the number one issue for all of 2026.

minimum_wage.stated_position: Schumer has repeatedly pushed to raise the federal minimum wage and was among Senate Democrats who sought to include the $15 minimum wage in the American Rescue Plan, vowing to find another path when it was ruled out of order.

student_debt.stated_position: Schumer is one of the most outspoken Senate proponents of student debt cancellation, co-leading efforts with Warren to push President Biden to cancel up to $50,000 in debt per borrower and fighting against Republican attempts to block relief.

housing.stated_position: Biden proposed a rent cap requiring corporate landlords (those owning 50+ units) to limit annual rent increases to 5% or lose federal tax depreciation incentives, while also calling on Congress to expand housing supply and provide mortgage relief for first-time homebuyers.
housing.direct_quote: My Administration is calling on Congress to pass legislation to limit rent increases at 5 percent for corporate landlords and take action to build and renovate more than 2 million affordable homes.

healthcare.stated_position: Biden supported expanding the Affordable Care Act, establishing a public insurance option, and lowering the Medicare enrollment age from 65 to 60, while stopping short of full single-payer Medicare for All.

aipac_bds.stated_position: Biden maintained strong support for U.S.-Israel military assistance and opposed the BDS movement, while also imposing financial sanctions on violent Israeli settlers in the West Bank—a step critics likened to aspects of the BDS framework.
aipac_bds.direct_quote: The situation in the West Bank—in particular high levels of extremist settler violence, forced displacement of people and villages, and property destruction—has reached intolerable levels.

minimum_wage.stated_position: Biden signed an executive order raising the federal contractor minimum wage to $15 per hour and repeatedly called on Congress to pass a nationwide $15 minimum wage, arguing that no one should work full time and still live in poverty.
minimum_wage.direct_quote: I signed an Executive Order today to raise the minimum wage for federal contractors to $15 per hour. No one working full-time should live in poverty.

student_debt.stated_position: Biden pursued multiple rounds of student debt cancellation, announcing up to $20,000 in relief for Pell Grant recipients (struck down by the Supreme Court in 2023) and ultimately canceling approximately $180 billion for nearly 5 million borrowers through targeted programs.
student_debt.direct_quote: Today, my Administration is cancelling student debt for 277,000 more people, bringing the total number of Americans who have been approved for debt relief so far under my Administration to 4.3 million borrowers through various actions.

housing.stated_position: Pelosi has supported Democratic housing affordability measures and criticized the Trump administration's cuts to housing programs, framing housing as a basic need that the government must protect.

healthcare.stated_position: Pelosi led the House passage of the Affordable Care Act in 2010 and has consistently defended it against Republican repeal efforts, championing expanded coverage and prescription drug price negotiation.

aipac_bds.stated_position: Pelosi is a strong supporter of U.S.-Israel ties and has attended and spoken at AIPAC conferences regularly, declaring the U.S.-Israel bond 'unbreakable' and criticizing the BDS movement as counterproductive and discriminatory.
aipac_bds.direct_quote: The bond between Israel and America is unbreakable. No one will succeed in making Israel a divisive issue. This commitment is honored in this Congress, where support for Israel remains unwavering and bipartisan.

minimum_wage.stated_position: Pelosi joined House Democrats in introducing the Raise the Wage Act of 2025 and previously passed $15 minimum wage legislation through the House twice as Speaker, calling the current wage 'corporate welfare' and vowing Democrats would persist.
minimum_wage.direct_quote: We will persist with the minimum wage. I've been fighting for this for years and quite frankly I think it should be higher.

housing.stated_position: AOC introduced the Homes Act (a $300 billion public housing investment) and the Place to Prosper Act (capping rent increases at 3% for large landlords), arguing that housing is a human right and that Wall Street investors are driving up costs.
housing.direct_quote: Every day, we see families grappling with the harsh reality of the housing crisis—skyrocketing rents, evictions, and homelessness—while big banks and Wall Street prioritize profit over people. The Homes Act is our bold response to this crisis, investing $300 billion over the next decade to ensure everyone can access an affordable home.

healthcare.stated_position: AOC is a leading co-sponsor of Medicare for All legislation and advocates for universal coverage as a human right, opposing any system that leaves Americans without healthcare due to cost.

aipac_bds.stated_position: AOC pledged she would not vote for any U.S. military aid to Israel, including defensive systems, and stated she has never voted to authorize funding to Israel, citing Israeli government violations of international law.
aipac_bds.direct_quote: I have not once ever voted to authorize funding to Israel, and I will never. The Israeli government should be able to finance their own weapons if they seek to arm themselves.

minimum_wage.stated_position: AOC has been a vocal supporter of raising the federal minimum wage to at least $15 an hour, sponsoring and co-sponsoring legislation to that effect and arguing current wages leave workers unable to meet basic needs.

student_debt.stated_position: AOC co-sponsored the Student Debt Cancellation Act to forgive all outstanding federal and private student loans for current and former students, arguing cancellation would provide a $1 trillion economic boost.

housing.stated_position: As California governor, Newsom signed significant housing legislation including the Live Local Act equivalent investments in California and championed zoning reform and affordable housing construction, while cutting housing budgets during state fiscal shortfalls.

healthcare.stated_position: Newsom signed legislation expanding Medi-Cal (California Medicaid) eligibility and a $25/hour minimum wage for healthcare workers, and has championed single-payer healthcare proposals in California.

aipac_bds.stated_position: Newsom declared he will 'never' accept AIPAC funding, questioned U.S. military aid to Israel, and suggested some people 'appropriately' describe Israel's conduct as apartheid—though he later expressed regret over the apartheid characterization.
aipac_bds.direct_quote: I will never accept AIPAC funding.

minimum_wage.stated_position: Newsom signed legislation raising the minimum wage for healthcare workers to $25 per hour and has supported California's broader minimum wage increases, making the state a leader on the issue nationally.

housing.stated_position: Buttigieg has spoken about the housing affordability crisis and criticized Trump's budget for cutting housing funding while increasing military spending, calling it unaffordable for American families.

healthcare.stated_position: During his 2020 presidential campaign, Buttigieg proposed 'Medicare for All Who Want It'—a public option that would allow Americans to choose between government-run and private insurance without eliminating private plans.

minimum_wage.stated_position: Buttigieg supported a $15 federal minimum wage during his 2020 presidential campaign and has not reversed that position, arguing it is essential for workers to earn a living wage.

student_debt.stated_position: During his 2020 presidential campaign, Buttigieg proposed making public college tuition-free for families earning up to $100,000, expanding public service loan forgiveness, and canceling debt for defrauded borrowers—while opposing blanket cancellation of all student debt.
